topic: P-wave shadow zone — EXP-200 non-incompatibility retreat preempt (post-integration adversarial review)
change_summary: Decider integrated EXP-200: appended a new 470-word paragraph to detail_extra titled "Closing the ‘non-incompatibility’ retreat (preemptive)". Paragraph argues (a) the retreat is "unavailable" because wins.html commits to a causal-mechanism verb ("creates"), and (b) "ineffective" because the dome’s own HOW-THIS-WORKS tolerance-band rule would then convert WIN-064 — and "every other N/A-tolerance domain" — into an unfalsifiable free-PASS, incompatible with the 94.5% accuracy figure. This c5 attacks the new paragraph only; the c4 holes on detail_verdict_text (live-fetch / paraphrased quote) are outside c5 scope and remain open in their own queue items.
our_argument_summary: Std Model Explains. Post-EXP-200, the Part 9 WIN-064 panel now closes both dome defenses the c3 advocate_mode rated defense_survives=3 — (a) radial-symmetry/fixed-wall (paragraph 1) and (b) non-incompatibility retreat (new paragraph 2). The core argument against the WIN is unchanged: the dome adopts a spherical-seismology result (104–140° shadow) without deriving it from disc geometry and with a monitoring panel that compares focal depth (km) against angular distance (degrees).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c4 — dome panel stills lists Tolerance: N/A + unit-mismatched Observed ("Confirmed at depth 378 km") + Status: PASS. EXP-200 does not alter the data or the live-panel analysis; it adds a dilemma argument on top of the c3 geometry refutation.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Unchanged — "hardcoded" tag is still the right call. Display panel, not live validator.
data_sources_used: USGS FDSN (claimed by dome panel; not independently verified via raw-text / monitor.py)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Tolerance: N/A persists. The new paragraph argues this IS the escape clause and converts it into a falsifiability violation against the dome’s own HOW-THIS-WORKS criterion — but only if the defender actually invokes the non-incompatibility retreat. A defender who holds the production-verb mechanism still has to defend it against paragraph 1’s azimuthal-symmetry attack.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None introduced by EXP-200. Residual c4 issue on detail_verdict_text live-fetch clause is unrelated to this queue item.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Unchanged from c4. 08-live-power.txt lines 244–246 show the P-wave Shadow Zone domain with Tolerance: N/A + static Observed: "Confirmed at depth 378 km" + Source: USGS FDSN + Status: PASS. Classic hardcoded-display signature — even if USGS FDSN is called, the Observed field is not dynamically bound to an angular shadow-zone measurement.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c4. 104–140° boundary = Oldham 1906 / Gutenberg 1913 textbook spherical seismology; dome takes the observation wholesale.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c4. Observation ~120 years old; WIN-064 is new in V51.0. Trivial-transcription under Part 3.1b.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c4. No raw-text derivation from disc_radius, H(r), or aetheric parameters produces 104–140°.

holes_found.description: Overreach on "every other N/A-tolerance domain." The new paragraph claims: "the retreat converts this domain — and, symmetrically, every other N/A-tolerance domain — into an unfalsifiable free-PASS that should not count toward the accuracy denominator." This paints ALL N/A-tolerance domains with one brush, but they are not a homogeneous set. Raw-text 08-live-power.txt lines 248–249 show CMB Axis of Evil with Predicted: >2σ | Observed: 2.5σ | Tolerance: N/A | Status: PASS — that is a THRESHOLD-style prediction (>2σ), not a non-incompatibility claim. An observation of 1.5σ would fall below the threshold and, by the dome’s own logic, would FAIL. N/A on the dome’s panels means "no proportional tolerance percentage," not "no falsifiability criterion." A sharp dome defender will point to CMB Axis of Evil (or any other threshold-style N/A panel) and say: "You claim every N/A domain is a free-PASS; here’s one with an explicit >2σ threshold that would clearly FAIL on a 1.5σ observation. You’ve conflated ‘non-proportional tolerance’ with ‘non-incompatibility claim’ — they’re not the same." That single counter-example lets the defender neutralise the sweeping scoreboard-disjunction move and retreat back to "WIN-064 alone is the contested case, and it carries a deterministic qualitative prediction (shadow at 104–140°), not a non-incompatibility claim." The dilemma still works for WIN-064 itself, but the "symmetrically" extension weakens the paragraph by inviting a concrete easy rebuttal.
holes_found.recommendation: Narrow the "every other N/A-tolerance domain" claim to "every other N/A-tolerance domain WHERE the panel lacks a threshold or categorical binary criterion" — or drop the symmetry extension entirely and keep the disjunction focused on WIN-064’s specific framing. Preferred rewrite: replace "this domain — and, symmetrically, every other N/A-tolerance domain — into an unfalsifiable free-PASS" with "this domain into an unfalsifiable free-PASS, alongside any other N/A-tolerance domain that similarly lacks a threshold or categorical criterion." This preserves the "some other domains fit the same critique" point (true for any panel whose N/A isn’t backed by a threshold) while not inviting the easy "but CMB has a >2σ threshold" counter.

holes_found.description: Raw-text archive does not contain the two dome quotes cited in the new paragraph. The paragraph treats as verbatim dome material: (a) "the Sub-Terrestrial Wall of the Closed Toroidal Ovoid creates a physical P-wave shadow zone between 104° and 140°… corresponding to the toroidal return-path boundary" (attributed to the public wins.html#win-064 entry); (b) "a physical structural boundary … where the aetheric return path meets the bottom plate of the ovoid cavity" (attributed to the Dome-vs-Globe row). Neither phrase appears anywhere in our raw-text/ archive — grep on "bottom plate", "ovoid cavity", "Closed Toroidal Ovoid", "toroidal return-path" across all raw-text files returns zero matches. The raw-text snapshot is V50.6 (dated 2026-03-12) and WIN-064 first appears in V51.0, so the quotes live only on the live dome site. If the dome author edits wins.html to soften "creates" or remove the ovoid-cavity language, our quoted material becomes unverifiable after the fact — and a defender can credibly say "show me in your archive where I said that." EXP-200 itself claims "verified 2026-04-21" but that assertion is in our expansion JSON, not in any archival snapshot a third party can audit. The detail_evidence field already carries one of these quotes (the "creates" quote, added in EXP-199), so this is not a new exposure — but EXP-200 compounds it by adding a second quote ("bottom plate") that is not in detail_evidence or raw-text, and by building the "Unavailable" half of the dilemma entirely on these two strings.
holes_found.recommendation: Two options, either acceptable. (a) Capture a Wayback Machine snapshot of john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html dated on or before 2026-04-21 and cite the Wayback URL alongside the john09289 URL in both quote citations — this makes the citation auditable even if the dome author edits later. (b) Fold a new raw-text snapshot of wins.html (call it raw-text/02b-wins-V51.txt or similar) into the repo and update monitor/poller so new V51-era dome content is archived going forward. Either fix closes the exposure. As a minimum stopgap (not a substitute for the above), add an inline "[verified via direct site fetch 2026-04-21]" annotation on each of the two quoted passages so a reader at least sees the verification claim in-place. Until one of these is done, the second hole (below) on the defender’s "you’re strawmanning me" move applies.

holes_found.description: Redundant preempt + overloaded "preemptive" label. detail_extra now has TWO paragraphs both framed as preempting dome defenses. Paragraph 1 (existing, radial-symmetry preempt) closes with: "The more honest dome reply is to drop the ‘confirmed’ label and restate WIN-064 as ‘disc cosmology is not logically incompatible with shadow zones’ — but a model that is merely not-incompatible with an observation makes no prediction, and the dome’s own scoreboard counts this as a confirmation, not a non-contradiction." That one sentence already disposes of the non-incompatibility retreat. Paragraph 2 (new EXP-200, 470 words) re-preempts the same retreat at length. The redundancy is not fatal — paragraph 2 adds the "94.5% accuracy" disjunction and the "Unavailable/Ineffective" framing, which paragraph 1 does not — but the repeat creates a structural issue a defender can exploit: "You claim to preempt this retreat twice, at different length, using different framings; evidently you weren’t confident your first preempt closed it. Why should I be?" Separately, both paragraphs label themselves "preempting" even though this WIN has been publicly published for multiple cycles and the dome author’s wins.html has been live for months — the retreat is not hypothetical future behaviour, it is a genuine live possibility. "Preempt" technically means "before it happens"; these are actually "rebut-before-invocation" moves.
holes_found.recommendation: Two light edits would resolve. (1) Trim the paragraph-1 closing sentence’s non-incompatibility clause to a shorter pointer toward paragraph 2 — e.g. replace the long sentence with "The more honest dome reply is to drop the ‘confirmed’ label and restate WIN-064 as ‘disc cosmology is not logically incompatible with shadow zones’; that retreat is addressed in the next paragraph." This hands off to paragraph 2 explicitly and eliminates the "we preempt the same thing twice" optic. (2) Change the paragraph-2 "(preemptive)" heading label to "(anticipated retreat)" or "(preempting the likely next move)" — both read as more defensible than claiming to preempt a publicly-live argument. Neither edit changes the substantive argument; both tighten the rhetoric.

holes_found.description: False-dichotomy risk in the closing disjunction. The paragraph ends: "either WIN-064 carries a quantitative prediction with a tolerance (in which case the depth-vs-angle category mismatch and missing tolerance are unresolved failures), or it is a compatibility statement (in which case it is withdrawn from the confirmed count along with every other N/A-tolerance domain in the same pipeline)." This is binary, but a dome defender has a clean third horn: "WIN-064 is a QUALITATIVE CATEGORICAL prediction with an implicit BINARY tolerance — a shadow observed in the angular range 104–140° counts as PASS, a shadow observed outside that range (or no shadow) counts as FAIL. N/A means ‘no proportional error-percentage’, not ‘no prediction’." Under that framing the dome panel’s display issue (showing focal depth in km rather than an angular band) becomes a presentation defect, not an unfalsifiability defect, and the compatibility-statement horn of our dilemma is declined. The paragraph later tries to close this exact third horn ("a genuinely qualitative test would omit the numbers and accept a shadow at any angle as confirmation, but the dome keeps the 104–140° boundary on the scoreboard"), but that closing attempt conflates two things: keeping the numeric boundary on the scoreboard does NOT preclude the prediction being categorical — the numbers are the band, and any shadow inside the band satisfies the categorical PASS. Our argument "you can’t ask for quantitative credit and qualitative falsifiability immunity on the same line" assumes the defender is asking for both; the defender can reasonably decline the quantitative credit and accept "this is just a categorical yes/no: shadow in range, yes, confirmed." That is fully consistent with the dome’s Tolerance: N/A = deterministic yes/no framing.
holes_found.recommendation: Strengthen the closing by engaging the categorical-binary reading directly. Add one sentence after "You cannot ask for quantitative credit and qualitative falsifiability immunity on the same line." Something like: "And even as a pure categorical test — shadow in the 104–140° band yes/no — the dome’s panel still does not display that comparison: the Observed field shows focal depth in kilometres, not an angular-shadow measurement. A categorical test that cannot display the category it tests is still a cosmetic indicator." That closes the third horn by showing it collapses into the depth-vs-angle unit mismatch we’re already arguing is unresolved — the dome can pick the categorical reading but then owes a panel that actually renders the category.

holes_found.description: TLDR does not mention the non-incompatibility argument. tldr_evidence reads: "The P-wave shadow zone … forms a symmetric ring around every earthquake worldwide — a pattern a fixed ‘Sub-Terrestrial Wall’ cannot produce … The dome adopts the observation while proposing a mechanism geometrically incompatible with what’s observed." That captures paragraph 1 (radial-symmetry / fixed-wall) but says nothing about paragraph 2 (non-incompatibility retreat). Post-EXP-200, the detail_extra has two load-bearing defenses; the TLDR advertises only one. This is not a TLDR factual error — the existing sentences are accurate — but it is an under-promise: a reader who reads only the TLDR now misses an argument we think is strong enough to deserve 470 words of detail. The curmudgeon.md severity rule treats TLDR factual errors as major and TLDR imprecision that doesn’t mislead as minor at most. This is imprecision, not error, so it lands at minor.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional. If desired, append one short clause to tldr_evidence: "… The dome adopts the observation while proposing a mechanism geometrically incompatible with what’s observed — and any retreat to ‘not incompatible’ concedes the mechanism without saving the confirmed badge." That adds ~20 characters and previews paragraph 2 without lengthening the TLDR into a summary. Skippable if editors prefer the TLDR to stay narrow.

stronger_arguments.description: The 94.5% disjunction is stronger if it is made PURELY WIN-specific and does not invoke the denominator. The current framing leans on "withdrawn from the confirmed count along with every other N/A-tolerance domain in the same pipeline" — that extension is what hole #1 flagged as over-reach. A cleaner and harder-to-deflect version is: "Invoking non-incompatibility forces the dome to pick which promise to keep — the scoreboard’s CONFIRMED badge (which assumes a production claim), or the underlying argument (which does not). The panel cannot keep both." This requires no sweeping count about other domains and no dependency on dome_claimed_accuracy at all; it attacks only the internal coherence of WIN-064’s own row. If editors want the 94.5% hook for rhetorical force, keep it as a secondary consequence rather than the load-bearing step.
sources: data/uncounted-failures.json (dome_accuracy_variants — dome’s own denominator variants disagree internally; 94.5%, 94.7%, 96.3%, 100.0%)
monitor/changes/chg-20260420-0414-001.json (poller: registry_ratio=94.5% on live site as of 2026-04-20)

stronger_arguments.description: The "Unavailable" half of the argument could be reinforced by citing the dome’s OWN methodology.json `timestamp_scope` concession (logged in monitor/analyst/human-notes.json around 2026-04-17 as the "API vs human text" gap) — the dome author has already quietly conceded on machine-readable files that the wins.html prose commits to claims the API’s scoped status does not support. That’s a self-consistency concession the author made himself, under duress from our review. Folding it in here would show a pattern: the dome author already understands the non-incompatibility retreat is the one he has to take; he has just not publicly taken it on wins.html yet.

kernel_of_truth.description: The new paragraph materially strengthens WIN-064 against the advocate’s strongest leg (non-incompatibility retreat rated defense_survives=3 in c3). The dilemma — production-claim vs compatibility-claim — is genuine and well-constructed; the dome author cannot publicly maintain both simultaneously. The "Ineffective" half of the argument (tolerance-band self-violation) is especially hard to dismiss because it uses the dome’s own HOW-THIS-WORKS block verbatim, which we have archivally verified against raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 163–174. The paragraph also correctly identifies that the 94.5% figure (poller-corroborated on the live site 2026-04-20) is load-bearing for the dome’s public case and cannot be defended under the compatibility-statement horn.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Kernel-of-truth here is not about the dome’s claim (the WIN is still Std Model Explains) but about the paragraph’s own strengths. The paragraph IS a real advance over paragraph 1 — the four holes above are edits, not a retraction. With the hole #1 narrowing, hole #2 archival fix, hole #3 label/redundancy trim, and hole #4 categorical-binary closing sentence, the paragraph becomes substantially harder to attack. None of the holes reaches verdict-change territory; the verdict stays Std Model Explains and the c4 recommendation to revisit the detail_verdict_text "live fetch" clause remains the separate open issue it was.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A capable dome defender post-EXP-200 would run three plays. (1) Concrete counter-example on the "every other N/A-tolerance domain" sweeping claim: "Point me to CMB Axis of Evil on the live-power panel — Predicted: >2σ, Observed: 2.5σ, Tolerance: N/A. That is a threshold test, not a non-incompatibility claim; a 1.5σ observation would FAIL. Your review paints all N/A panels as free-PASSes; I just showed you one that isn’t. Your scoreboard dilemma collapses to WIN-064 specifically, and for that single panel I simply accept the categorical-binary horn you tried to close." (2) Archive-walk on the quoted material: "Show me in your raw-text archive where I used the phrase ‘bottom plate of the ovoid cavity’ or ‘Closed Toroidal Ovoid creates a physical P-wave shadow zone’. You can’t — your archive is V50.6 from March, those are V51-era strings. You’re citing content you did not preserve; nothing stops me from editing wins.html and you from having nothing to compare against." (3) Categorical-binary horn on the closing disjunction: "WIN-064 is a qualitative categorical prediction. N/A means ‘no proportional tolerance’, not ‘no prediction’. Shadow observed in 104–140° range = PASS; outside = FAIL. The depth-vs-angle display issue is a panel presentation bug, not a falsifiability defect." Leg (1) is the hardest-hitting and is why hole #1 is rated major; leg (2) is why hole #2 is rated major; leg (3) is why hole #4 is rated moderate. With the four recommended edits, all three legs lose their footholds: the sweeping claim is narrowed, the citations become archivally auditable, and the categorical-binary horn is closed by the extra sentence about panel display. defense_survives drops from 3 to 1 (leg 3 residual only, partially closed by the depth-vs-angle mismatch argument the paragraph already carries).
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Apply hole #1 + hole #4 edits in a single patch cycle — they are the highest-leverage. Hole #2 (archival verification) is worth doing but can be done separately since it requires new infrastructure (Wayback capture or raw-text snapshot extension). Hole #3 (redundancy trim) is cosmetic and can be bundled with any future touch of the detail_extra. Hole #5 (TLDR) is skippable.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No cross-WIN inconsistencies introduced by EXP-200. The non-incompatibility dilemma framing is WIN-064-specific; it does not propagate to WIN-067, WIN-063, or WIN-054, none of which have an N/A-tolerance panel with a unit-mismatched Observed field. However: hole #1 ("every other N/A-tolerance domain") technically gestures at CMB Axis of Evil, which belongs to no WIN currently (checked via grep of wins.json for "CMB Axis" and "Axis of Evil" — appears only in Part 2b prose and in dome raw-text/08-live-power.txt, not as a WIN subject). So the over-reach does not create a live cross-WIN inconsistency, but it does create an adjacency to prose we do not control. Hole #1’s narrowing recommendation also protects against future WIN onboarding picking up CMB Axis of Evil as its own WIN subject.

claims_checked: "Sub-Terrestrial Wall" / "bottom plate of the ovoid cavity" appear in our raw-text
"every other N/A-tolerance domain" is a defensible sweeping claim
HOW-THIS-WORKS tolerance-band quote verbatim against raw-text
quantitative_verification.computations: Grep across raw-text/*.txt for "Sub-Terrestrial", "Sub-terrestrial", "bottom plate", "ovoid cavity", "Closed Toroidal Ovoid", "toroidal return-path" returns zero matches — the V50.6-dated raw-text snapshot does not contain these V51-era strings. 94.5% IS a live-site dome figure per monitor/changes/chg-20260420-0414-001.json ("registry_ratio=94.5%" observed 2026-04-20). N/A-tolerance panels in raw-text/08-live-power.txt: P-wave Shadow Zone (WIN-064) AND CMB Axis of Evil — the latter has an explicit >2σ threshold with 2.5σ observed, so it is threshold-style, not non-incompatibility-style. The HOW-THIS-WORKS tolerance-band quote matches raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 165 verbatim. detail_extra is 4310 chars, 606 words across 2 paragraphs post-EXP-200 (vs 218 words pre-patch per EXP-200 metadata).
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Two dome-quoted phrases ("Sub-Terrestrial Wall … creates" and "bottom plate of the ovoid cavity") are attributed to live wins.html but not found in our archive. Not a factual discrepancy with the dome site — our expansion says the quotes were verified 2026-04-21 — but a verifiability gap against our own repo. Addressed in hole #2.

deeper_analysis:
EXP-200 is a good expansion — it takes the c3 advocate’s strongest surviving leg (non-incompatibility retreat, defense_survives=3) and builds a genuine dilemma around two public commitments the dome author has made (the "creates" mechanism verb on wins.html and the tolerance-band rule on live.html). The dilemma structure is the right move: unlike paragraph 1 (radial-symmetry), which demands a specific geometric rebuttal the dome doesn’t yet have, the paragraph 2 dilemma forces a choice between two public claims the author has already endorsed. That’s harder to retreat from.

The four content holes I’m flagging are edits to make the dilemma sharper, not structural flaws. Hole #1 (N/A-tolerance over-reach) is the most important because it hands the defender a concrete easy rebuttal in CMB Axis of Evil; narrow the sweep and that leg disappears. Hole #2 (archive verifiability) is a standing exposure that applies to any citation of V51-era dome content from our V50.6 snapshot — worth fixing infrastructure-wide, not just for this WIN. Hole #3 (preempt redundancy) is cosmetic but creates a "two preempts for the same retreat" optic that’s worth tightening. Hole #4 (categorical-binary third horn) is a real gap in the closing disjunction; one extra sentence closes it.

Importantly, this c5 does NOT touch the c4 residual on detail_verdict_text (the "live fetch call" clause adjacent to the "hardcoded" tag). That remains a separate open issue on its own queue item and is out of scope here. It should NOT be re-flagged as new — decider has it queued.

Verdict stays "Std Model Explains"; no basis for verdict_change. recommended_action = minor_edit (four edits as specified in holes_found). advocate_mode.defense_survives at 2 given that leg (1) is the major open vulnerability, leg (2) is the archive-walk exposure, and leg (3) is moderate; with the recommended edits, defense_survives drops to 1.

P-wave Shadow Zone panel Tolerance: N/A + Observed: "Confirmed at depth 378 km" (raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 244–246) — verbatim match
CMB Axis of Evil panel Predicted: >2σ, Observed: 2.5σ, Tolerance: N/A (raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 248–249) — verbatim match
Dome 94.5% claimed accuracy on live site (monitor/changes/chg-20260420-0414-001.json, poller observed 2026-04-20) — corroborated
EXP-200 appended paragraph present in wins.json WIN-064 detail_extra (length 4310 chars, 606 words) — integration verified via direct read
citations_failed: "Sub-Terrestrial Wall of the Closed Toroidal Ovoid creates a physical P-wave shadow zone between 104° and 140°…" — attributed to wins.html#win-064; NOT present in any raw-text/*.txt (archive is V50.6, WIN-064 is V51-era). Hole #2.
"a physical structural boundary … where the aetheric return path meets the bottom plate of the ovoid cavity" — attributed to Dome-vs-Globe row; NOT present in any raw-text/*.txt. Hole #2.
citations_unchecked: EXP-200 metadata claim "verified 2026-04-21" — not independently re-verified via live fetch in this review; raw-text archive was used as the adversarial baseline. A live-fetch re-verification would close hole #2 if the quotes are still on the live site; it would also escalate hole #2 to critical if they are not.
